CLAUDE.md

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Package Overview

ebirdst is an R package for accessing and analyzing eBird Status and Trends data products from the Cornell Lab of Ornithology. It provides tools for downloading, loading, and analyzing species distribution models, population trends, and habitat associations.

Common Commands

# Document (regenerate NAMESPACE and man/)
devtools::document()

# Build and check
devtools::check()

# Run all tests
devtools::test()

# Run a single test file
devtools::test(filter = "loading")   # runs tests/testthat/test_loading.R

# Run tests with output
testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test_loading.R")

# Install locally
devtools::install()

# Build pkgdown site
pkgdown::build_site()

Tests require EBIRDST_KEY set in the environment (or .Renviron) for download tests. Many tests use skip_on_cran() and skip_if_offline().

Architecture

Data Flow

set_ebirdst_access_key()    # stores key in ~/.Renviron as EBIRDST_KEY
        ↓
ebirdst_download_status() / ebirdst_download_trends()
        ↓   downloads to {EBIRDST_DATA_DIR}/{version}/{species_code}/
load_raster() / load_trends() / load_config() / etc.
        ↓   returns terra::SpatRaster or data frames
grid_sample() / rasterize_trends() / calculate_mcc_f1() / etc.

Key Abstractions

  • Species codes: 6-letter eBird codes (e.g., "yebsap" for Yellow-bellied Sapsucker). get_species() resolves common names, scientific names, or codes to the canonical code.
  • Data directory: Controlled by EBIRDST_DATA_DIR env var. Structure: {base_dir}/{version_year}/{species_code}/. Access via ebirdst_data_dir() and get_species_path().
  • Raster products: Weekly layers (52 per year) returned as terra::SpatRaster. Products include abundance, occurrence, count, and habitat associations.
  • Stixels: Spatiotemporal subregions used in model fitting. load_stixels() / stixelize() work with these.

File Organization (R/)

File Responsibility
download.R ebirdst_download_*() functions; handles API auth and file retrieval
load.R load_*() functions for every product type; largest file
sample.R grid_sample(), grid_sample_stratified(), assign_to_grid() for spatiotemporal sampling
trends.R rasterize_trends(), vectorize_trends(), convert_ppy_to_cumulative()
ebirdst-palettes.R ebirdst_palettes(), abundance_palette() for visualization
utils.R date_to_st_week(), calculate_mcc_f1(), validation helpers
access-key.R set_ebirdst_access_key() and internal get_ebirdst_access_key()
data.R Documentation for built-in datasets (ebirdst_runs, ebirdst_predictors)

Built-in Reference Data

Three data frames shipped with the package (generated by scripts in data-raw/): - ebirdst_runs: ~3,000 species with model metadata and quality ratings - ebirdst_predictors: 150+ predictor variables used in models - ebirdst_predictor_descriptions: Metadata for predictor datasets

Testing Patterns

  • tests/testthat/setup.R downloads example data to a temp directory before tests run
  • Use skip_on_cran() for any test requiring network or local data files
  • Use skip_if(nchar(Sys.getenv("EBIRDST_KEY")) == 0) to skip download tests without a key
  • withr::defer() handles cleanup in setup

Documentation

All user-facing functions use roxygen2 (#') with @param, @return, @examples. Regenerate with devtools::document(). Five vignettes in vignettes/ cover status data, applications, trends, the API, and the changelog.

On this page

Developed by Matthew Strimas-Mackey, Shawn Ligocki, Tom Auer, Daniel Fink.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
